message:
fix(core): reject read() on directory inodes with IsDirectory error

Add an is_dir() guard in the Ext4 read path of FsOps::read() to
return FfsError::IsDirectory before attempting to read file data.
Without this check, calling read() on a directory inode would
attempt to interpret directory entries as file content, producing
garbage output instead of the expected EISDIR error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
